method_note: Signal-replay partition through backtrader bt.Strategy contract (mirrors V10 precedent). The 100% apparent divergence vs in-house is artifactual: per-window n_trades=0-1 prevents meaningful Sharpe calculation in the framework partition; the backtrader adapter reports 0.0 because there are too few trades per fold for a non-zero std. This is NOT a clean implementation-gap measurement — a full event-driven vectorbt/backtrader port (as done for SMA-34405 bb_reversion × vectorbt) would be required for genuine framework parity.
parity_note: Both engines partition identically to in-house walk_forward.json. Per-fold partition is correct; Sharpe magnitude differs because Sharpe is unstable at n_trades=1.

w5_verdict.rationale: 100% abs rel divergence > 50% threshold. Note: the measured divergence reflects signal starvation (n_trades=13 total → 0-1 trades per fold) rather than an implementation gap; the strategy is already NOT-PROFITABLE in-sample so W5 auto-archive aligns with the existing verdict.
